title: veronica cilicica var. surculosa
intro: Veronica cilicica var. surculosa, also known as Turkish speedwell, is a low-growing, woody-stemmed perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Turkey. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic and to reduce fever, which is attributed to its high concentration of iridoid glycosides and other bioactive compounds. Consuming Turkish speedwell is believed to provide various health benefits, including improved urinary tract health, reduced inflammation, and enhanced antioxidant defenses, making it a potential natural remedy for various health conditions. Turkish speedwell can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is often used in traditional medicine, particularly in Turkey and other parts of Europe.
